prompt: Is the variant in the following VCF record a SNP or an indel? How can you tell from the REF and ALT fields?
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_090
chr06	28410583	.	TAGC	T	1920	PASS	AF=0.11;AN=6020	GT:DP:GQ	0/1:22:65
correct_answer: Indel (specifically a 3-bp deletion). REF=TAGC (4 bases) and ALT=T (1 base), so the variant deletes AGC. A SNP would have REF and ALT of equal length (both 1 base).
rubric: Must identify as indel/deletion. Must note the length difference between REF and ALT. Must state it is a 3-bp deletion (AGC deleted).
notes: Basic variant type classification from REF/ALT lengths.
